marketFocus: Iran Lays Mines in Strait of Hormuz — Escalation Resumes: CBS reported US intelligence sees Iran taking steps to deploy mines in the Strait of Hormuz; CNN confirmed Iran has begun laying mines. US destroyed 10 inactive mine-laying boats. Trump warned 'military consequences at a level never seen before' if mines not removed. WTI settled $83.45/bbl (down from $119 peak but Hormuz disruption looks set to stay). DXY strengthened on haven bid. US CPI today 13:30 Frankfurt — critical catalyst.

RBA Rate Hike 76% Probability — AUD Outperforms: NAB and Westpac now expect RBA to hike in BOTH March and May. Goldman Sachs calls March hike. RBA Deputy Governor Hauser: 'there will be a very genuine policy debate.' Money markets: 76% likelihood of 25bps increase next week. AUD/USD outperformed all G10 peers — range 0.7053-0.7126. ASX 200 gained with mining, resources, materials and financials leading.

IEA Proposes Largest Ever Oil Reserve Release — No Decision Yet: WSJ reported IEA proposed largest ever release of oil from strategic reserves (exceeding 182mln bbls released in 2022 for Russia-Ukraine). IEA meeting ended Tuesday with no decision. Countries decide today. G7 Energy Ministers confirmed readiness to act. Saudi Aramco CEO: ~180mln bbls disruption so far, 2mln BPD spare capacity could be restored within days.

USD.summary: DXY strengthened as disruptions to flows through the Strait of Hormuz look set to stay. The CBS report that Iran is taking steps to deploy mines was the catalyst — reversing Tuesday's risk-on move and reinstating the geopolitical haven premium. US CPI today at 13:30 Frankfurt is the key data catalyst: a hot print (+0.3%+ MoM) would reinforce the stagflation narrative and accelerate USD strength; a soft print could temporarily pressure DXY. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: DXY strengthened — Hormuz mine threat reinstates haven premium
Iran mine deployment: CBS + CNN confirmed, Hormuz disruption looks set to stay
US CPI today 13:30 Frankfurt — hot print = stagflation narrative, USD positive
US Existing Home Sales Feb 4.09M (exp. 3.89M) — beat, economy resilient
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

EUR.summary: EUR/USD barely retained the 1.1600 level — support held but price action is contained and the structural bearish case remains intact. German CPI Feb Final came in exactly in-line (0.2% MoM, 1.9% YoY) — no positive surprise. ECB speakers sent mixed signals: Kazimir says hike 'closer than thought' but not at next week's meeting; Villeroy says no hike next week; Nagel says 'will act decisively if energy spike feeds into durably higher inflation'; Lagarde says 'cannot say what they will do.' The ECB uncertainty combined with the Eurozone's energy importer status keeps EUR under pressure. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: EUR/USD barely held 1.1600 — structural bearish case intact
German CPI Feb Final 0.2% MoM / 1.9% YoY — in-line, no positive surprise
ECB mixed signals: Kazimir (hike closer), Villeroy (no hike next week), Lagarde (uncertain)
Eurozone energy importer — Hormuz mine threat = persistent stagflation headwind
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

GBP.summary: GBP swung between gains and losses on Tuesday, ultimately weakening as BoE rate hike bets were trimmed after the pullback in oil prices. The dominant driver of GBP's recent strength — BoE easing priced out — is now being partially reversed as oil retreats from $119/bbl peaks. GBP/USD remained confined within the prior day's range at the 1.3400 handle. No UK-specific data today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment. GBP is in a wait-and-see mode ahead of Thursday's BoE Governor Bailey speech.
drivers: GBP swung between gains/losses — ultimately weakened as BoE hike bets trimmed
Oil pullback reduces BoE hawkish repricing that drove GBP last week
GBP/USD confined to 1.3400 range — no directional conviction
BoE Governor Bailey speaks Thursday — key risk event for GBP
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

JPY.summary: USD/JPY returned to 158.00 territory as the dollar strengthened on Hormuz mine reports. Japanese PPI Feb showed a surprise monthly deflation (-0.1% MoM vs +0.1% exp) — this delays the BoJ's case for further rate hikes. ING wrote that BoJ is likely to stand pat in April, favouring a June hike instead. Japan's government is considering measures amid Middle East tensions and will announce gas and utility price measures at an appropriate time. Japan 5yr JGB auction was strong (b/c 3.69x vs prev. 3.10x). JPY is bearish but the Hormuz escalation provides some haven support at the margin. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY back to 158.00 — dollar strengthened on Hormuz mine reports
Japan PPI Feb -0.1% MoM (exp. +0.1%) — deflation surprise delays BoJ hike
ING: BoJ likely stands pat in April, June hike favoured instead
Japan 5yr JGB auction strong (b/c 3.69x vs prev. 3.10x) — demand intact
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment

AUD.summary: AUD was the clear G10 outperformer — range 0.7053-0.7126. Multiple factors converged: NAB and Westpac now expect RBA to hike in both March and May; Goldman Sachs calls a March hike; money markets price 76% probability of 25bps next week; RBA Deputy Governor Hauser said 'there will be a very genuine policy debate.' ASX 200 gained with mining, resources, materials and financials leading. China Trade beat (Tuesday) and base metals strength (copper +) add to the bullish case. AUD is Daily Bullish but Weekly Neutral — no High Conviction alignment, but strong intraday case.
drivers: AUD/USD outperformed all G10 — range 0.7053-0.7126
NAB + Westpac: RBA hike March AND May — Goldman Sachs confirms March call
Money markets: 76% probability of 25bps RBA hike next week
RBA Deputy Hauser: 'very genuine policy debate' at next meeting
ASX 200 gained — mining, resources, materials, financials led

CAD.summary: WTI settled $83.45/bbl on Tuesday — still significantly elevated above pre-war levels ($70s). CAD remains the G10 petro-currency beneficiary. Canadian government is seeking options to boost oil production — a supply-side positive that adds to CAD's structural advantage. The Loonie was the best overnight performer on oil surge, then pared as oil pared. G7 Energy Ministers confirmed readiness to act on SPR release — a potential mild CAD headwind if oil falls further, but the level remains supportive. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI $83.45/bbl settlement — still elevated, CAD petro-advantage intact
Canadian government seeks options to boost oil production — supply positive
G7 Energy Ministers: readiness to act on SPR release — watch for oil pressure
Loonie best overnight performer on oil surge — pared as oil pared
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

CHF.summary: CHF faces competing forces: Hormuz mine threat reinstates haven demand, but APAC stocks traded higher (risk-on recovery) which caps CHF upside. SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 remains in place. Swiss data calendar is empty today. CHF is Weekly Weak Bullish but Daily Neutral — no High Conviction alignment. The haven trade is mixed as geopolitical escalation and risk-on recovery alternate. CHF will likely follow the oil narrative today — Hormuz escalation = CHF bid, IEA SPR release = CHF offered.
drivers: Hormuz mine threat reinstates haven demand — CHF bid
APAC stocks higher — risk-on recovery caps CHF upside
SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 — limits CHF appreciation
Empty Swiss data calendar — entirely geopolitics-driven
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

NZD.summary: Antipodeans gained but NZD underperformed AUD — the RBA hike story is AUD-specific and NZD lacks its own catalyst. China Trade beat (Tuesday) provides some support as China is NZD's largest trading partner, but the RBNZ remains dovish and there is no domestic data catalyst. China vehicle sales Feb fell 15.2% YoY (vs -3.2% in Jan) — a negative signal for NZD. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment. NZD is in a holding pattern — follow AUD directionally but with less conviction.
drivers: Antipodeans gained but NZD underperformed AUD — RBA story is AUD-specific
China vehicle sales Feb -15.2% YoY (vs -3.2% Jan) — negative for NZD
RBNZ dovish — no domestic rate hike catalyst unlike RBA
China Trade beat (Tuesday) provides some support — China = NZD's largest partner
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes settled 3 ticks higher at 112-14+ on Tuesday but faced headwinds from record corporate issuance (Amazon $25-30bln, Salesforce $25bln, Honeywell $16bln — fresh record for single-day corporate bond issuance). Short-end yields picked up sharply after the CBS Hormuz mine report reinstated inflation fears. US CPI today at 13:30 Frankfurt is the key catalyst — a hot print would send yields higher and reinforce the stagflation narrative. Treasury futures are lacklustre amid oil price swings and supply pressure.
drivers: T-notes settled 3 ticks higher but short-end yields spiked on Hormuz mine report
Record corporate issuance: Amazon + Salesforce + Honeywell = USD 66bln+ single day
US CPI today 13:30 Frankfurt — hot print = yields higher, stagflation narrative
3yr Treasury auction was very weak — demand concerns at front end
Hormuz mine threat reinstates inflation fears — bond negative

bonds.name: German Bund 10Y
bonds.summary: Bund futures traded subdued but found support near the 127.00 level. German CPI Feb Final came in exactly in-line (0.2% MoM, 1.9% YoY) — no surprise. Germany sold EUR 3.871bln vs EUR 5bln 2.10% 2028 Schatz with a weak b/c of 1.61x (prev. 1.77x) — poor demand. ECB speakers sent mixed signals on rate hike probability. EUR 10bln of issuances scheduled this week. Bunds are in a holding pattern ahead of today's US CPI which will set the tone for global yields.
drivers: Bund futures subdued — found support near 127.00
German CPI Feb Final in-line (0.2% MoM, 1.9% YoY) — no surprise
Germany Schatz auction weak: b/c 1.61x (prev. 1.77x), yield 2.72% (prev. 2.02%)
ECB mixed signals — Kazimir (hike closer), Villeroy (no hike next week)
US CPI today will set global yield direction

bonds.name: JGB 10Y
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures were initially pressured by positive APAC risk appetite but then gradually recovered, facilitated by a strong 5yr JGB auction (b/c 3.69x vs prev. 3.10x, yield 1.633% vs prev. 1.640%). Japan PPI Feb -0.1% MoM (deflation surprise) delays BoJ hike case — ING now expects BoJ to stand pat in April and hike in June instead. JGBs are supported by the delayed BoJ normalisation narrative and strong domestic demand at auctions.
drivers: JGB futures recovered after initial pressure — strong 5yr auction (b/c 3.69x)
Japan PPI Feb -0.1% MoM (exp. +0.1%) — deflation surprise delays BoJ
ING: BoJ stands pat in April, June hike favoured instead
Positive APAC risk appetite initially pressured JGBs but recovered
Japan government considering gas and utility price measures — fiscal support
